name: OverTheWire Bandit — Ongoing Training
who: Every new VISI member. If you've never touched a Linux terminal before, this is your starting point — no exceptions.
how: Go to https://overthewire.org/wargames/bandit and SSH into each level. Work through it at your own pace. Bring questions to weekly meetings.
deliverables: Core Linux command-line fluency that every other competition assumes you have. Bandit is the foundation everything else is built on.

name: picoCTF picoGym — Weekly Practice
who: All members. Beginners start with Easy challenges; intermediate members push into Medium. This is VISI's standard weekly training resource.
how: Create a free account at https://play.picoctf.org. The club runs a shared leaderboard — DM an officer your username to get added. Aim for 1–2 challenges per week.
deliverables: Hands-on reps in web exploitation, cryptography, reverse engineering, forensics, and binary exploitation. Progress is visible on the leaderboard.

name: US Cyber Challenge: Cyber Quests
who: Any VISI member who wants a free shot at earning a SANS Cyber Camp invitation. No experience required — the challenges scale from easy upward.
how: Register at https://www.uscyberchallenge.org. Complete the online Cyber Quests challenges (Feb–Apr window). Top performers receive invitations to free week-long Cyber Camps with SANS instructors.
deliverables: Free SANS training (if you place high enough), resume credential, and a benchmark of your current skill level against students nationwide.

name: National Cyber League — Fall 2026 Season
who: All VISI members. NCL is calibrated from easy to hard — there is no skill floor. Every member should compete. VISI fields as many teams of 7 as we can.
how: Registration opens at https://nationalcyberleague.org. Pay the $45 fee (talk to leadership about club subsidies). Three phases: Gymnasium (practice) → Individual Game → Team Game. Check #visi-ncl for team assignments.
deliverables: A personal Scouting Report documenting your skills — employers recognize this. School ranking on a national leaderboard. NCL is the single most resume-impactful competition VISI participates in.

name: DOE CyberForce — Applications Open
who: Intermediate and advanced members comfortable with system administration and defense. Ideal for members who've completed at least one NCL season.
how: Applications open mid-July at https://cyberforce.energy.gov. VISI will submit one team application. Express interest in #cyberforce-interest by July 1 so leadership can assemble the roster.
deliverables: Real-world critical infrastructure defense experience, DOE recognition, and strong resume material for ICS/OT security roles.

name: CSAW CTF Qualifier — NYU
who: Intermediate and advanced members. If you've placed in the top half of NCL or completed 10+ HackTheBox/TryHackMe rooms, this is your next step.
how: Register a team at https://csaw.io. 48-hour online jeopardy-style CTF. VISI fields one primary team; solo competitors can also register individually. Coordination in #csaw-2026.
deliverables: Exposure to elite-level CTF challenges, potential advancement to NYU in-person finals, and recognition in one of the world's largest student security events (3,000+ participants).

name: NCL Fall — Individual Game
who: All registered NCL members. This phase is solo — your score is yours alone and feeds your personal Scouting Report.
how: Log into your NCL account at https://nationalcyberleague.org when the Individual Game window opens. 9 challenge categories. Play at any time during the window.
deliverables: Personal Scouting Report with skill percentile rankings. This document goes directly on your resume and LinkedIn.

name: Cybersecurity Awareness Month — VISI Campus Events
who: All members. This is VISI's most visible public-facing month. We run workshops, tabling events, and talks for the broader UTRGV campus.
how: Watch #announcements for the October event schedule. Volunteer to present, staff tables, or just show up and bring friends. We're a registered CISA Cybersecurity Awareness Month partner — your participation builds the club's reputation.
deliverables: Community impact, campus visibility for VISI, and leadership/speaking experience for members who run events.

name: Hivestorm — UTSA CIAS
who: All members, especially those preparing for CCDC. Hivestorm is explicitly designed for students who've never competed in a defensive competition before.
how: Registration opens at https://hivestorm.org. VISI will field unlimited teams — every interested member gets a spot. You'll be given Windows and Linux VMs to secure against a scoring engine. No prior experience required.
deliverables: Hands-on blue team experience: hardening systems, removing malware, and fixing misconfigurations under time pressure. Direct preparation for CCDC.

name: NCL Fall — Team Game
who: All registered NCL members. Team rosters will be posted in #ncl-teams. You'll compete as a unit and contribute to UTRGV's school ranking.
how: Log into NCL and join your assigned team. Coordinate with teammates in your team's Discord thread. Communicate — challenges are faster when you split work by category.
deliverables: School ranking on the national NCL leaderboard. Team experience that shows up in your Scouting Report. Best NCL finish of the year.

name: CPTC — Registration Opens
who: Advanced members only. CPTC requires strong offensive security skills — experience with Metasploit, manual exploitation, and professional report writing. Talk to Damian if you're unsure whether you're ready.
how: Registration at https://cp.tc. VISI submits one team application. Regional qualifier is virtual in fall; global finals are in-person in spring. Express interest in #cptc-interest immediately — spots are limited.
deliverables: A real penetration test engagement, a professional-grade pentest report, and a simulated executive debrief. Past competitors have discovered real-world CVEs. Elite resume credential.

name: DOE CyberForce Competition
who: The VISI CyberForce team (selected in July). If you missed July signup, watch for waitlist openings in #cyberforce-2026.
how: Log into the CyberForce platform on competition day. Defend critical infrastructure systems while red teamers attack. Maintain uptime of required services while hardening your environment.
deliverables: DOE recognition, critical infrastructure defense experience, and strong differentiation for ICS/OT security job applications.

name: SANS Holiday Hack Challenge
who: All members. The Holiday Hack is narrative-driven and fun — it's designed to be played over the holidays at your own pace. Perfect for beginners wanting their first CTF win.
how: Register at https://www.sans.org/mlp/holiday-hack-challenge. Opens mid-November, submissions close in January. VISI runs a holiday hack thread in #ctf-general for hints and collaboration.
deliverables: SANS recognition for top writeups, community fun, and a low-pressure first CTF experience. Top submissions get featured by SANS publicly.

name: National Cyber League — Spring 2027 Season
who: All members. Spring NCL is your chance to improve your Fall score, climb the school leaderboard, and get a second Scouting Report showing growth.
how: Registration opens at https://nationalcyberleague.org in January. Same format as Fall: Gymnasium → Individual → Team Game. Returning members should aim to improve on their Fall category scores.
deliverables: Updated Scouting Report showing skill progression since Fall. Stronger school ranking. A second resume credential showing consistent competition engagement.

name: NCAE Cyber Games — Qualifying Window
who: Members who have never competed in a defensive competition — NCAE is explicitly designed for first-timers. If Hivestorm was your first competition, this is your next step.
how: Register at https://ncaecybergames.org. VISI fields multiple teams. Qualifying runs Jan–Feb, regionals in March, nationals in spring. Teams defend systems while maintaining services.
deliverables: NSA-backed recognition, regional and national placement, and blue team experience that builds directly toward CCDC.

name: CCDC Southwest — Qualifier
who: The selected VISI CCDC team of 8. Team will be finalized by December 2026. If you want to be considered, talk to Damian now and start attending CCDC practice sessions.
how: Virtual qualifier through https://southwestccdc.com. Team defends a live corporate network against professional red teamers while keeping business services running. Practice begins December 2026 — weekly drills mandatory.
deliverables: Advancement to SW Regionals in Tulsa if you place. The most realistic simulated SOC experience available to collegiate students.

name: TexSAW CTF — UT Dallas
who: All members. TexSAW is explicitly designed for beginners and is one of the most welcoming Texas CTFs. Send as many teams as you want.
how: Registration at https://texsaw.utdallas.edu when it opens (typically Jan/Feb). Form teams in #ctf-signups. 48-hour jeopardy-style challenges.
deliverables: First or early CTF experience, Texas cybersecurity community networking, and placement on CTFtime.org for VISI's team profile.

name: US Cyber Challenge: Cyber Quests
who: Any member — especially those who didn't participate in the Spring 2026 window. Top performers get free SANS Cyber Camp invitations.
how: Register at https://www.uscyberchallenge.org. Complete challenges during the Feb–Apr window.
deliverables: Potential free SANS training, national skill benchmark, and resume credential.

name: picoCTF 2027 — Carnegie Mellon
who: All members. picoCTF is the world's largest free cybersecurity CTF — 50,000+ participants. VISI fields as many teams of 5 as possible.
how: Register at https://picoctf.org when registration opens (typically Jan/Feb for March competition). Join #picoctf-2027 for team coordination. 10-day window, play any time.
deliverables: National placement and ranking, hands-on CTF experience across all major categories, and a CMU-backed credential. Top VISI finisher gets club recognition.

name: UTCTF 2027 — UT Austin
who: All members. UTCTF is one of the best Texas CTFs — free, well-organized, and has beginner tracks alongside advanced challenges.
how: Registration at https://utctf.live. Form teams in #ctf-signups. Coordinate in #utctf-2027 during the event.
deliverables: CTF placement, Texas cybersecurity community presence, and practice across web, crypto, rev, pwn, and forensics categories.

name: TAMUctf 2027 — Texas A&M
who: All members. TAMUctf is student-built and has a reputation for creative challenges. Great for intermediate members who've already done TexSAW and UTCTF.
how: Registration at https://tamuctf.com. Form teams in #ctf-signups.
deliverables: CTF experience, Texas A&M community networking, and placement in another major Texas competition.

name: CCDC Southwest — Regionals
who: The VISI CCDC team that advances from the February qualifier. In-person in Tulsa, OK.
how: Travel coordination via #ccdc-team. ~32 teams compete. Two-day in-person competition defending a full corporate network. Flights/lodging logistics TBD based on budget.
deliverables: Regional placement, potential advancement to nationals in San Antonio, and the most credentialed defensive competition win available to collegiate students.

name: NCAE Cyber Games — Regionals
who: VISI teams that qualified from the January window.
how: Virtual competition through https://ncaecybergames.org. Teams defend systems while red teams attack. Top regional teams advance to spring nationals.
deliverables: NSA-backed recognition and advancement to nationals.

name: CCDC National Championship
who: The VISI CCDC team, if UTRGV advances from SW Regionals. In-person, San Antonio area — close to home.
how: National competition. Details at https://nationalccdc.org. Top 10 regional teams from across the US compete for the national title.
deliverables: National recognition, recruiter visibility (major security companies sponsor CCDC), and the most prestigious blue team credential in collegiate cybersecurity.

name: NCAE Cyber Games — Nationals
who: VISI teams that advanced from regionals.
how: Virtual national finals at https://ncaecybergames.org.
deliverables: NSA-backed national recognition and placement. Excellent first-competition credential for members who've never competed before.

name: CPTC Global Finals
who: The VISI CPTC team that advances from the fall qualifying round.
how: In-person finals. Details at https://cp.tc. Full penetration test of a target organization with report delivery and executive presentation.
deliverables: Global placement in the premier collegiate pentesting competition, professional pentest report (portfolio piece), and direct recruiter exposure from sponsoring companies.

name: NCL Spring 2027 Season — Closes
who: All registered NCL Spring 2027 members.
how: Final week of Team Game. Push hard on remaining challenges. Final Scouting Reports are generated after the season closes.
deliverables: Final updated Scouting Report for the academic year. Every registered member walks away with a resume-ready skills document.
